q1.question: What is Nodeflare?
q1.answer: A dedicated hosting service for MCP. Auto-convert any stdio-format MCP server from GitHub to SSE format and deploy. Fine-grained access token permissions and OAuth supported. Most features are free.

q2.question: What's the difference between stdio and SSE?
q2.answer: stdio is for local execution and doesn't log who called what or when. SSE format accesses via HTTP, so all requests are logged and per-token permission control is possible. Effective for preventing internal MCP leakage.

q3.question: What are the benefits for enterprises?
q3.answer: With SSE format, 'who called which tool and when' is fully logged. Audit logs impossible with stdio enable instant detection and response if internal MCPs leak externally.

faq.title: Billing FAQ
faq.cancelQuestion: How do I cancel my subscription?
faq.cancelAnswer: Click 'Manage Subscription' to access the billing portal where you can cancel. You'll keep access until your billing period ends.
faq.changeAnswer: Yes! You can upgrade or downgrade anytime. Upgrades are prorated. Downgrades take effect at the end of your current billing period.
faq.paymentAnswer: We accept major credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express) through Stripe, our secure payment processor.

faqPage.title: FAQ
faqPage.subtitle: Frequently asked questions about Nodeflare.
faqPage.notFound: Can't find what you're looking for? Feel free to

basic.a1: Nodeflare is a cloud hosting service for MCP servers. MCP servers allow AI assistants like Claude to access external tools and data. Simply connect your GitHub repository and builds and deployments happen automatically.
basic.a2: An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server defines tools that AI assistants can use. For example, you can provide database queries, file operations, external API calls, and any other functionality to AI.
basic.q3: Which AI can I use this with?
basic.a3: Currently, we support Anthropic's Claude. You can use it with Claude Desktop, Claude.ai, or via API — any MCP-compatible environment.

pricing.q1: What can I do with the free plan?
pricing.a1: The free plan includes up to 3 servers and 10,000 requests per month. This is sufficient for personal use and small projects.
pricing.q2: When should I upgrade to a paid plan?
pricing.a2: Consider upgrading to Pro (¥2,980/month) if you need more than 4 servers, exceed 10,000 monthly requests, or require custom domains and priority support.
pricing.a3: We accept credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, JCB). Contact us if you need invoice billing.

technical.a1: We support TypeScript/JavaScript, Python, Go, and Rust. Custom containers using Dockerfile are also available.
technical.a2: Typically, deployment completes within 1-2 minutes after pushing to GitHub. The first build may take slightly longer due to dependency installation.
technical.q3: How do I set environment variables?
technical.a3: You can securely set environment variables from each server's settings page in the dashboard. Sensitive information like API keys and secrets are stored encrypted.
technical.a4: Custom domains are available on Pro plan and above. SSL certificates are automatically issued after DNS configuration.

security.a1: You can issue access tokens for each server. Permissions can be set per token, and tokens can be revoked anytime when no longer needed.
security.a2: Yes, you can view logs for all tool invocations from the dashboard. This includes request content, responses, and execution times.
security.a3: Servers run in the Tokyo region, and data is stored domestically in Japan.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Free for small projects. Scale as you grow.
pricing.enterpriseCustomPricing: Custom pricing based on your needs

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: Is it easy to upgrade from the free plan?
faq.a1: Yes, you can upgrade with one click from your dashboard. Your data and settings will be preserved.
faq.a2: We accept credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, JCB).
faq.a3: Yes, you can cancel anytime. You'll continue to have access until the end of your billing period.
faq.q4: What happens if I exceed my request limit?
faq.a4: We'll notify you as you approach your limit. If exceeded, additional charges may apply or requests may be temporarily throttled.
faq.q5: How much do I save with yearly billing?
faq.a5: You save about 17% with yearly billing. For example, the Pro plan is ¥2,980/month, but with yearly billing it's about ¥2,483/month equivalent.

docs.title: NodeFlare User Guide
docs.subtitle: Learn how to use each screen and feature of NodeFlare.

contact.prompt: Can't find what you're looking for?

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: The first screen displayed after login. View server status and announcements at a glance.
features: Server Cards: View each server's status (Running/Stopped/Error) at a glance
Quick Actions: Restart or deploy servers with one click
Usage: Current month's request count, server count, etc.
actions: Click server card → Navigate to server details
"New Server" button → Navigate to server creation
Restart icon → Restart the server

servers.title: Server List
servers.description: Displays all created MCP servers in a list.
features: Server Name: Click to navigate to details
Status Badge: Running (green) / Stopped (gray) / Error (red) / Building (yellow)
Last Deploy: Most recent deployment date/time
Action Buttons: Quick actions like restart, stop, deploy
actions: "New Server" button → Navigate to server creation
Click server row → Navigate to server details
Workspace switcher → Switch between workspaces using the selector at the top

serverNew.title: Create Server
serverNew.description: Screen for creating a new MCP server. Select a GitHub repository to deploy.
features: Server Name (Required): Display name for management
Slug (Required): Identifier used in URL (lowercase letters and hyphens only)
GitHub Repository (Required): Select the repository to deploy from
Root Directory (Optional): For monorepos, the directory containing server code
actions: Enter server name and slug
Click "Select Repository" to choose a GitHub repository
Change branch if needed
Click "Create Server" - build and deployment will start automatically

serverDetail.title: Server Details
serverDetail.description: Displays basic server information and current status. Includes Overview, Deployments, Test, Secrets, Webhooks, Metrics, and Settings tabs.
features: Endpoint URL: URL for connecting from AI clients
Last Deploy: Most recent deployment date/time and commit hash
Available Tools: List of MCP tools provided by this server
actions: "Copy" button → Copy endpoint URL to clipboard
"Deploy" button → Execute manual deployment
"Restart" button → Restart the server
"Stop" button → Stop the server

logs.title: Request Logs
logs.description: View MCP server request logs in real-time.
features: Server Select: Switch which server to display
Search: Filter by tool name or response
Live Mode: Toggle auto-refresh ON/OFF (2-second interval)
actions: Live button → Toggle auto-refresh ON/OFF
Refresh button → Manually refresh logs
Download button → Export logs as PDF

accessTokens.title: Access Tokens
accessTokens.description: Manage tokens used to access MCP servers. Tokens are displayed only once when created.
features: Target Server: All servers or specific server
Scopes: Full Access / Tools / Resources / Prompts
actions: "New Token" button → Create new access token
Copy the displayed token and store it securely
Delete unused tokens

oauth.title: OAuth Apps
oauth.description: Manage OAuth clients for external applications to access MCP servers.
features: App Name: Name of the OAuth client
Redirect URIs: Multiple redirect URIs can be registered
Client ID: Used in OAuth authorization requests
Client Secret: Used for authentication at token endpoint
actions: "New Client" button → Create new OAuth client
Register redirect URIs and allowed scopes
Client ID and Client Secret will be issued

team.title: Team Members
team.description: Invite members to your workspace to collaboratively manage servers.
features: Member Count: Current member count and limit
Member List: Each member's name, email, and role
actions: "Add Member" button → Invite new member by email
Select role for the invited member
Change role via dropdown (except for owner)
"Delete" button → Remove member from workspace

billing.title: Billing
billing.description: Manage subscriptions and view billing information.
features: Current Plan: Plan name, price, and next billing date
Usage: Server count, request count, etc.
Payment Methods: Credit card registration and management
Billing History: List of past invoices
actions: "Change Plan" button → Upgrade or downgrade subscription
Register or change credit card
Download PDF receipts for each invoice

settings.title: User Settings
settings.description: Manage account settings and notification preferences.
features: Notifications: Deploy success/failure, server down, weekly report
actions: Change display name and email
Toggle notification settings ON/OFF
Sign out or delete account
